summary_one_line: Resolving the false side requires a content-type string that does not exactly equal the literal "multipart/form-data".

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A Content-Type header value reaching content_type_match must be a string that differs from the exact literal "multipart/form-data" so the strcmp-style comparison fails and the false branch is taken; winners carry a parsed Content-Type field (e.g. "Content-Type:where") that is non-empty but is NOT the magic literal.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds embed an explicit "Content-Type:" header byte run at offsets 0x24-0x2b (47 43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 = 'GContent', then 'Type:where'), so cthdr is set, the loop at L2381 advances past it, contenttype is non-null yet not equal to multipart/form-data, and content_type_match returns false (the blocked F side). The I2S substitution stage planted the comparison-relevant string content that distinguishes this branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds lack the parsed Content-Type field: at L2380 if(cthdr) is False (T=0 F=20), so the else-if at L2383 sets cthdr = "multipart/form-data" verbatim, and at the blocker L1897 content_type_match is True (T=20 F=0) — the loser is pinned to the magic literal and can only ever take the True side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer from the content_type_match comparison and substitutes operand bytes, manufacturing a Content-Type value that satisfies the if(cthdr) path while differing from the multipart/form-data literal; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and never construct the required header byte sequence within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a content_type_match equality test against the literal multipart/form-data and the globally blocked side is the false branch.
Both decisive pairs differ exactly by the I2S technique, with cmplog and value_profile_cmplog resolving 10/10 while naive and value_profile block 10/10.
Winner seeds carry an explicit Content-Type header byte run.
Winner Content-Type value is a non-multipart string such as where.
When the loser has no cthdr the caller substitutes the literal multipart/form-data verbatim before the blocker.
At the call site the loser misses the Content-Type and is forced onto the multipart default.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L2380  T=20 F=0  T=0 F=20  if(cthdr)

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker the loser takes only the True side while the winner takes only the False side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L1897  T=0 F=20  T=20 F=0  if(content_type_match(contenttype, STRCONST("multipart/fo...

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 279 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 3ada62fdafca01cd` and observed two I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 18 and 19 of the ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the comparison target is content_type_match against multipart/form-data shows naive/value_profile reaching the false (non-matching) side at the same rate as cmplog/vpc, indicating the divergence is not due to I2S operand substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff at offsets 0x24-0x2c is only flagged PARTIAL/DIFFER rather than a clean concentrated W vs L magic-literal split, so the exact operand I2S substitutes into the Content-Type field is inferred from source flow more than from a sharp byte signature.
